text.title: Project Documentation Convention Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Repo has CLAUDE.md with 400 lines of architecture. Agent SDK agents see nothing.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated CLAUDE.md as the single-source-of-truth.
antipattern.fix: Move content to AGENTS.md (essential) + `.agents/` (deep). CLAUDE.md becomes one line: `@AGENTS.md`.

antipattern.symptom: AGENTS.md is 350 lines. Every agent load burns 8K tokens of context.
antipattern.fix: Cap at 80 lines; move detail to `.agents/<topic>.md`. AGENTS.md links by relative path.

antipattern.symptom: Repo has root CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md but subpackages have none. Agents working in subpackages re-read the whole repo every time.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought repo-root docs were enough.
antipattern.fix: Per-module coverage rule: every source dir carries the pair. Validator flags missing pairs.

antipattern.symptom: AGENTS.md is a free-form paragraph. CI cannot validate it.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought the convention was prose-only.
antipattern.fix: Required headings: dir purpose, file table, key types/commands, gotchas. Validator regex-checks.
